file.content:
# `cc-metric-store` version 0.1.1

This is a bugfix release of `cc-metric-store`, the metric timeseries cache
implementation of ClusterCockpit.
For release specific notes visit the [ClusterCockpit Documentation](https://clusterockpit.org/docs/release/).

Notable changes in version 0.1.0:

- Cleanup of code and restructuring
- Document REST API with Swagger
- Introduce REST API versioning
- Provide Swagger UI test web-frontend
- Introduce re-sampling of metric data
- Support also ms, ns in line protocol
- Support NATS credentials

## Breaking changes

None

1→# ClusterCockpit Metric Store
     2→
     3→[![Build & Test](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-metric-store/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-metric-store/actions/workflows/test.yml)
     4→
     5→The cc-metric-store provides a simple in-memory time series database for storing
     6→metrics of cluster nodes at preconfigured intervals. It is meant to be used as
     7→part of the [ClusterCockpit suite](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit). As all
     8→data is kept in-memory, accessing it is very fast. It also provides topology aware
     9→aggregations over time _and_ nodes/sockets/cpus.
    10→
    11→There are major limitations: Data only gets written to disk at periodic
    12→checkpoints (or via WAL on every write), not immediately as it is received.
    13→Only the configured retention window is kept in memory.
    14→Still metric data is kept as long as running jobs is using it.
    15→
    16→The storage engine is provided by the
    17→[cc-backend](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend) package
    18→(`cc-backend/pkg/metricstore`). This repository provides the HTTP API wrapper.
    19→
    20→The [NATS.io](https://nats.io/) based writing endpoint and the HTTP write
    21→endpoint both consume messages in [this format of the InfluxDB line
    22→protocol](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-specifications/blob/master/metrics/lineprotocol_alternative.md).
    23→
    24→## Building
    25→
    26→`cc-metric-store` can be built using the provided `Makefile`.
    27→It supports the following targets:
    28→
    29→- `make`: Build the application, copy an example configuration file and generate
    30→  checkpoint folders if required.
    31→- `make clean`: Clean the golang build cache and application binary
    32→- `make distclean`: In addition to the clean target also remove the `./var`
    33→  folder and `config.json`
    34→- `make swagger`: Regenerate the Swagger files from the source comments.
    35→- `make test`: Run tests and basic checks (`go build`, `go vet`, `go test`).
    36→
    37→## Running
    38→
    39→```sh
    40→./cc-metric-store                              # Uses ./config.json
    41→./cc-metric-store -config /path/to/config.json
    42→./cc-metric-store -dev                         # Enable Swagger UI at /swagger/
    43→./cc-metric-store -loglevel debug              # debug|info|warn (default)|err|crit
    44→./cc-metric-store -logdate                     # Add date and time to log messages
    45→./cc-metric-store -version                     # Show version information and exit
    46→./cc-metric-store -gops                        # Enable gops agent for debugging
    47→```
    48→
    49→## REST API Endpoints
    50→

file.content:
# ClusterCockpit Metric Store

[![Build & Test](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-metric-store/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-metric-store/actions/workflows/test.yml)

The cc-metric-store provides a simple in-memory time series database for storing
metrics of cluster nodes at preconfigured intervals. It is meant to be used as
part of the [ClusterCockpit suite](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit). As all
data is kept in-memory, accessing it is very fast. It also provides topology aware
aggregations over time _and_ nodes/sockets/cpus.

There are major limitations: Data only gets written to disk at periodic
checkpoints (or via WAL on every write), not immediately as it is received.
Only the configured retention window is kept in memory.
Still metric data is kept as long as running jobs is using it.

The storage engine is provided by the
[cc-backend](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend) package
(`cc-backend/pkg/metricstore`). This repository provides the HTTP API wrapper.

The [NATS.io](https://nats.io/) based writing endpoint and the HTTP write
endpoint both consume messages in [this format of the InfluxDB line
protocol](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-specifications/blob/master/metrics/lineprotocol_alternative.md).

## Building

`cc-metric-store` can be built using the provided `Makefile`.
It supports the following targets:

- `make`: Build the application, copy an example configuration file and generate
  checkpoint folders if required.
- `make clean`: Clean the golang build cache and application binary
- `make distclean`: In addition to the clean target also remove the `./var`
  folder and `config.json`
- `make swagger`: Regenerate the Swagger files from the source comments.
- `make test`: Run tests and basic checks (`go build`, `go vet`, `go test`).

## Running

```sh
./cc-metric-store                              # Uses ./config.json
./cc-metric-store -config /path/to/config.json
./cc-metric-store -dev                         # Enable Swagger UI at /swagger/
./cc-metric-store -loglevel debug              # debug|info|warn (default)|err|crit
./cc-metric-store -logdate                     # Add date and time to log messages
./cc-metric-store -version                     # Show version information and exit
./cc-metric-store -gops                        # Enable gops agent for debugging
```

## REST API Endpoints

content.thinking:
Now let me look at the key changes to understand what to put in the release notes. Let me look at the significant commits:

1. `b06bb58 Add entire integration` - Integrates the entire cc-backend
2. `0fa5d8b Run go fix tool. Refactor` - Code refactoring
3. `5547580 Update to lates cc-backend head`
4. `b764120 Integrate entire. Upgrade dependencies`
5. `6aca448 Port to newest cclib. Use metricstore as library.` - Major change: metricstore now used as library from cc-backend


6. `dd23f49 Add configuration options and enable dynamic memory management through cc-backend callback`
7. `be6d4be Fix healthcheck api`
8. `e2e4dcd feat: HealthCheck endpoint for nodes` - New feature
9. Avro checkpoint format (multiple commits)
10. `5be7578 Bump github.com/nats-io/nats.go from 1.36.0 to 1.47.0`
11. `fc4ae14 Bringing back GOGC for syncPool`

The major themes are moving the storage engine to cc-backend as a library, adding configuration and memory management capabilities, introducing a new HealthCheck endpoint for nodes, switching to Avro checkpoint format, and updating key dependencies like NATS while optimizing garbage collection.

content.thinking:
Now I have enough information to write a comprehensive release notes update. The next version should be 0.2.0 given the significant architectural changes:

1. **Major architectural change**: The storage engine (previously internal `memorystore`) has been extracted and moved to `cc-backend/pkg/metricstore` as a library. This is a breaking change as the entire internal implementation was removed.

2. **New features**:
   - HealthCheck endpoint for node health status (`GET /api/healthcheck/`)
   - Dynamic memory management via cc-backend callback
   - Avro checkpoint format support
   - GOGC management for sync pool
   
3. **Breaking changes**: The internal `memorystore`, `avro`, `resampler`, and `util` packages have been removed entirely, with the storage engine now sourced from `cc-backend/pkg/metricstore`, and the configuration schema has been adapted accordingly.

4. **Improvements**: I've refactored the startup code, enhanced configuration schema validation, improved error handling throughout, and cleaned up the codebase with `go fix`.

5. **Dependency updates**: The nats.go library has been upgraded from 1.36.0 to 1.47.0, along with various other dependency improvements.

Now I'm putting together the release notes for v0.2.0.

content.text: Now I have a clear picture of all changes since v0.1.1. Let me write the updated release notes for v0.2.0.

input.content:
# `cc-metric-store` version 0.2.0

This is a major release of `cc-metric-store`, the metric timeseries cache
implementation of ClusterCockpit.
For release specific notes visit the [ClusterCockpit Documentation](https://clusterockpit.org/docs/release/).

## Breaking changes

- The internal `memorystore`, `avro`, `resampler`, and `util` packages have been
  removed. The storage engine is now provided by the
  [`cc-backend`](https://github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend) package
  (`cc-backend/pkg/metricstore`). This repository is now the HTTP API wrapper
  only.
- The configuration schema has changed. Refer to `configs/config.json` for the
  updated structure.

## Notable changes

- **Storage engine extracted to `cc-backend` library**: The entire in-memory
  time-series storage engine was moved to `cc-backend/pkg/metricstore`. This
  reduces duplication in the ClusterCockpit suite and enables shared maintenance
  of the storage layer.
- **HealthCheck API endpoint**: New `GET /api/healthcheck/` endpoint reports the
  health status of cluster nodes.
- **Dynamic memory management**: Memory limits can now be adjusted at runtime via
  a callback from the `cc-backend` library.
- **Configuration schema validation**: The config and metric config JSON schemas
  have been updated and are now validated against the structs they describe.
- **Startup refactored**: Application startup has been split into `cli.go` and
  `server.go` for clearer separation of concerns.
- **`go fix` applied**: Codebase updated to current Go idioms.
- **Dependency upgrades**: `nats.go` bumped from 1.36.0 to 1.47.0;
  `cc-lib` updated to v2.8.0; `cc-backend` updated to v1.5.0; various other
  module upgrades.

---

# `cc-metric-store` version 0.1.1

This is a bugfix release of `cc-metric-store`, the metric timeseries cache
implementation of ClusterCockpit.
For release specific notes visit the [ClusterCockpit Documentation](https://clusterockpit.org/docs/release/).

Notable changes in version 0.1.0:

- Cleanup of code and restructuring
- Document REST API with Swagger
- Introduce REST API versioning
- Provide Swagger UI test web-frontend
- Introduce re-sampling of metric data
- Support also ms, ns in line protocol
- Support NATS credentials

## Breaking changes

None

content.content: The file /Users/jan/prg/cc-metric-store/ReleaseNotes.md has been updated successfully.

This is a bugfix release of `cc-metric-store`, the metric timeseries cache
implementation of ClusterCockpit.
For release specific notes visit the [ClusterCockpit Documentation](https://clusterockpit.org/docs/release/).

Notable changes in version 0.1.0:

content.text:
The `ReleaseNotes.md` has been updated with a new v0.2.0 section. Here's a summary of what was added:

**Breaking changes:**
- Internal `memorystore`, `avro`, `resampler`, and `util` packages removed — the storage engine is now sourced from `cc-backend/pkg/metricstore`
- Configuration schema has changed

**Notable changes:**
- Storage engine extracted to `cc-backend` as a library (the biggest architectural shift — ~3000 lines removed from this repo)
- New `GET /api/healthcheck/` endpoint for node health status
- Dynamic memory management via cc-backend callback
- Updated and validated config/metric JSON schemas
- Refactored startup code (`cli.go` / `server.go` split)
- `go fix` applied throughout
- Dependency upgrades: nats.go 1.36→1.47, cc-lib v2.8.0, cc-backend v1.5.0
